qa_pairs.question: What types of resources are available for researchers to access through the Resource Allocation Competition?
qa_pairs.answer: Researchers can request access to general-purpose systems, large shared memory systems, general-purpose computing on graphics processing units, storage, and cloud resources.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of technical support is offered with an allocation?
qa_pairs.answer: Our team of experts can assist researchers with installing and configuring software packages, optimizing code, and solving technical issues. This support is generally limited to problems requiring a few days of effort, not deep 'embedded' programming support over several months.

qa_pairs.question: Is it recommended to consult with technical staff before submitting a competition application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is strongly recommended that all applicants consult with technical staff responsible for the clusters they intend to use. This ensures the technical aspects of the proposals align with the requested clusters. Consultations should occur in advance of completing the proposal to allow for discussion or revisions. If you need assistance finding the appropriate technical staff, you can email allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: How does a researcher apply to the competition?
qa_pairs.answer: Any researcher interested in applying to the RAC must first be registered with the CCDB. All applications are then submitted electronically through the CCDB’s Resource Allocation Competition page.

qa_pairs.question: Can a researcher apply for both the Resources for Research Groups (RRG) and the Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) processes?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. A Principal Investigator (PI) can apply as a lead submitter for only one RRG application but can participate in multiple RRG submissions. PIs may also simultaneously hold lead positions on one or several RPP projects, but still only one RRG project.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I have questions after reviewing the competition guide?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have questions or concerns after reviewing the competition guide, please contact allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Is the Canadian Common CV (CCV) required for RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Principal Investigators (PIs) and all Co-PIs of a RAC application are required to submit a CCV. This helps standardize CV formats and assists Scientific Review Committees in evaluating the quality of the research team.

qa_pairs.question: How can Co-PIs update their CCV on the CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: Co-PIs can update their CCV by clicking the "Update CCV" button on the Resource Applications page, or by navigating to 'My Account' → 'View Reporting'. Once updated, the CCV status will automatically refresh in the RAC online application form.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a glossary for acronyms or terms used in RAC documentation?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a Technical Glossary is available that lists key terms used in the competition and application process. Applicants should consult it when preparing their application.

qa_pairs.question: Will my project proposal be at a disadvantage if it doesn't fully rely on existing infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: The RAC exclusively grants access to advanced research computing (ARC) infrastructure. If you have questions about whether the infrastructure you are using is part of the national platform, you should contact allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Can a small research team or one with limited funding still apply to the RAC?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, academic PIs can apply for an allocation regardless of the size of their research team or the amount of funding they have received.

qa_pairs.question: Are extensions available for RAC application submission deadlines?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the submission deadline for RAC applications is final. No extensions will be granted, and late submissions will not be accepted. Any concerns regarding this policy can be directed to allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: When will applicants be notified of their RAC application results?
qa_pairs.answer: All applicants will be notified of their competition results in March via email. After all recipients have been privately notified, a list of recipients, project allocation amounts, and allocation values will be published on the RAC Annual Reports page.
